BitBucketCloudCommit.description: A BitBucketCloud specific implementation of a git commit.

author.description: The author of the commit, assumed to be the person who wrote the code.

date.description: When the commit was committed to the project, in ISO 8601 format

activities.description: The activities such as OPENING, CLOSING, MERGING or UPDATING a pull request

comments.description: The comments on the pull request

commits.description: The commits associated with the pull request

metadata.description: The pull request and repository metadata

created_on.description: When the comment was created, in ISO 8601 format

updated_on.description: When the comment was updated, in ISO 8601 format

created_on.description: When the pr was created, in ISO 8601 format

participants.description: People who have participated in the PR

source.description: The PR's source, The repo Danger is running on

updated_on.description: When the pr was updated, in ISO 8601 format

approved.description: Did they approve of the PR?

account_id.description: The acount id of the commit author

display_name.description: The display name of the commit author

nickname.description: The nick name of the commit author

uuid.description: The uuid of the commit author

BitBucketServerCommit.description: A BitBucketServer specific implementation of a git commit.

author.description: The author of the commit, assumed to be the person who wrote the code.

displayName.description: The display name of the commit author

emailAddress.description: The email of the commit author

name.description: The id of the commit author

authorTimestamp.description: The UNIX timestamp for when the commit was authored

committer.description: The author of the commit, assumed to be the person who committed/merged the code into a project.

displayName.description: The display name of the commit committer

emailAddress.description: The email of the commit committer

name.description: The id of the commit committer

committerTimestamp.description: When the commit was committed to the project

displayId.description: The shortened SHA for the commit

activities.description: The activities such as OPENING, CLOSING, MERGING or UPDATING a pull request

comments.description: The comments on the pull request

commits.description: The commits associated with the pull request

metadata.description: The pull request and repository metadata

BitBucketServerPRDSL.description:
An exact copy of the PR's reference JSON. This interface has type'd the majority
of it for tooling's sake, but any extra metadata which BitBucket Server send
will still be inside the JS object.

createdDate.description: Date PR created as number of milliseconds since the unix epoch

participants.description: People who have participated in the PR

toRef.description: The repo Danger is running on

updatedDate.description: Date PR updated as number of milliseconds since the unix epoch

approved.description: Did they approve of the PR?

key.description: The project's human readable project key

scmId.description: The type of SCM tool, probably "git"

displayName.description: The name to use when referencing the user

type.description: The type of a user, "NORMAL" being a typical user3

CliArgs.description:
Describes the possible arguments that
could be used when calling the CLI

base.description: The base reference used by danger PR (e.g. not master)

dangerfile.description: A custom path for the dangerfile (can also be a remote reference)

externalCiProvider.description: Used by danger-js o allow having a custom CI

id.description: So you can have many danger runs in one code review

textOnly.description: Use SDTOUT instead of posting to the code review side

DangerDSLJSONType.description: The root of the Danger JSON DSL.

bitbucket_cloud.description: The data only version of BitBucket Cloud DSL

bitbucket_server.description: The data only version of BitBucket Server DSL

git.description: The data only version of Git DSL

github.description: The data only version of GitHub DSL

gitlab.description: The data only version of GitLab DSL

settings.description:
Used in the Danger JSON DSL to pass metadata between
processes. It will be undefined when used inside the Danger DSL

cliArgs.description:
This is still a bit of a WIP, but this should
pass args/opts from the original CLI call through
to the process.

github.description:
Saves each client re-implementing logic to grab these vars
for their API clients

accessToken.description: API token for the GitHub client to use

additionalHeaders.description: Optional headers to add to a request

GitCommit.description: A platform agnostic reference to a Git commit

tree.description: Potential parent commits, and other assorted metadata

name.description: The display name for the author

GitHubAPIPR.description: Provides the current PR in an easily used way for params in `github.api` calls

GitHubCommit.description: A GitHub specific implementation of a git commit, it has GitHub user names instead of an email.

author.description: The GitHub user who wrote the code

committer.description: The GitHub user who shipped the code

parents.description: An array of parent commit shas

url.description: the url for the commit on GitHub

GitHubIssue.description:
This is `danger.github.issue` It refers to the issue that makes up the Pull Request.
GitHub treats all pull requests as a special type of issue. This DSL contains only parts of the issue that are
not found in the PR DSL, however it does contain the full JSON structure.

A GitHub Issue

labels.description: The labels associated with this issue

color.description: The color associated with this label

id.description: The identifying number of this label

url.description: The URL that links to this label

commits.description: The github commit metadata for a code review session

issue.description: The issue metadata for a code review session

pr.description: The PR metadata for a code review session

requested_reviewers.description: The people/teams requested to review this PR

reviews.description: The reviews left on this pull request

thisPR.description: The PR metadata specifically formatted for using with the GitHub API client

label.description: The human display name for the merge reference, e.g. "artsy:master"

ref.description: The reference point for the merge, e.g. "master"

repo.description: The repo from whch the reference comes from

sha.description: The reference point for the merge, e.g. "704dc55988c6996f69b6873c2424be7d1de67bbe"

user.description: The user that owns the merge reference e.g. "artsy"

GitHubPRDSL.description:
An exact copy of the PR's reference JSON. This interface has type'd the majority
of it for tooling's sake, but any extra metadata which GitHub send will still be
inside the JS object.

additions.description: The number of additional lines in the PR

assignee.description: The User who is assigned the PR

assignees.description: The Users who are assigned to the PR

author_association.description: How does the PR author relate to this repo/org?

body.description: The markdown body message of the PR

changed_files.description: The number of changed files in the PR

closed_at.description: optional ISO6801 Date string for when PR was closed

comments.description: The number of comments on the PR

commits.description: The number of commits in the PR

created_at.description: ISO6801 Date string for when PR was created

deletions.description: The number of deleted lines in the PR

html_url.description: The link back to this PR as user-facing

locked.description: Has the PR been locked to contributors only?

merged.description: Has the PR been merged yet?

merged_at.description:
Optional ISO6801 Date string for when PR was merged.
Danger probably shouldn't be running in this state.

review_comments.description: The number of review-specific comments on the PR

updated_at.description: ISO6801 Date string for when PR was updated

user.description: The User who submitted the PR

assignee.description: Is someone assigned to this PR?

assignees.description: Are there people assigned to this PR?

description.description: The textual description of the repo

full_name.description: The full name of the owner + repo, e.g. "Danger/Danger-JS"

html_url.description: The root web URL for the repo, e.g. https://github.com/artsy/emission

name.description: The name of the repo, e.g. "Danger-JS"

GitHubReview.description:
GitHubReview
While a review is pending, it will only have a user.  Once a review is complete, the rest of
the review attributes will be present

body.description: If there is a review, the body of the review

commit_id.description: If there is a review, the commit ID this review was made on

id.description: If there is a review, this provides the ID for it

state.description:
The state of the review
APPROVED, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT or PENDING

user.description: The user requested to review, or the user who has completed the review

avatar_url.description: The url for a users's image

href.description: The href for a users's page

type.description: Whether the user is an org, or a user

GitJSONDSL.description: The Git Related Metadata which is available inside the Danger DSL JSON

created_files.description: Newly created filepaths relative to the git root

deleted_files.description: Removed filepaths relative to the git root

modified_files.description: Filepaths with changes relative to the git root

commits.description: All the individual commits in the merge request

JIRAIssue.description: This is `danger.bitbucket_server.issues` It refers to the issues that are linked to the Pull Request.

key.description: The unique key for the issue e.g. JRA-11

url.description: The user-facing URL for that issue

pullRequestID.description: The ID for the pull/merge request "11"

description:
The shape of the JSON passed between Danger and a subprocess. It's built
to be expanded in the future.
